title: Distinct Characters Subsequence

content.problemStatement:
You have initially a string of **N** characters, denoted by **A1,A2...AN**. You have to print the size of the largest subsequence of string **A** such that all the characters in that subsequence are distinct ie. no two characters in that subsequence should be same.

A subsequence of string **A** is a sequence that can be derived from **A** by deleting some elements  and without changing the order of the remaining elements.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a non‑empty string S consisting of printable ASCII characters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single integer – the size of the largest subsequence of S that contains only distinct characters.

sampleTestCases.explanation: All three characters are different, so the whole string can be taken as the subsequence.
The character 'a' appears twice; we can keep one 'a' and the 'b', giving a subsequence of length 2.
All characters are distinct, therefore the answer equals the string length.

solution.approach: The required subsequence can contain at most one occurrence of each character. Therefore the answer equals the number of distinct characters present in the string. We simply scan the string once, marking each character in a boolean array of size 256. Whenever we encounter a character for the first time we increment a counter. This yields the answer in O(|S|) time per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the string. Overall O(total length of all strings).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – a fixed array of 256 booleans.

    # Simple deterministic generator for the hidden test cases defined above.
    cases = [
        "1
a",
        "1
 ",

aaa",
        "1
ABCDEFGHIJKLMNO",

        "1
~!@#$%^&*()_+{}|",
        "1
abababababababa",
        "1
bbbbbbbb",
        "1
1111111",
        "1
abcdefghijklmno",
        "1
onmlkjihgfedcba",
        "2
abc
cba",
        "3
a
ab
abc",
        "1
ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ",
        "1
A1B2C3D4E5",

abc
aaa
abcd
abca

        "4
hello
world
code
chef",
        "2
hello world
foo bar",
        "3
!@#
$$$%
&&&&"
    ]
    print(cases[i])
